title: Calostephane huillensis
taxonomy: Calostephane huillensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Calostephane. The plant's classification is further detailed as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, and order Asterales. Calostephane huillensis is native to Mozambique. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

It is often utilized to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The plant's roots and leaves are commonly used in traditional medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and other remedies. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal benefits and confirm its effectiveness in treating various ailments.
